 {"id":"bd-2nkmi.5","title":"Interactive controls + keybindings","description":"What
- Implement interactive controls and keybindings as defined in the spec.

Must support
- Sample navigation, re-render, zoom/fit, layout mode cycle, metrics/control toggles, fidelity/glyph/wrap/style toggles.
- Help overlay that lists the full keybinding map.

UX requirements
- On-screen legend is always discoverable (footer or overlay).
- Controls remain usable at 80x24 and richer at 120x40/200x60.

Acceptance
- Keybindings are implemented once, exercised by unit tests, and mirrored in help text.

Implemented Esc key handling (CollapsePanels action collapses controls + metrics panels) and added 50+ unit tests exercising all keybinding mappings, state transitions, zoom clamping, mode cycling, panel toggling, epoch tracking, and sample navigation. Quality gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings all pass. 62 mermaid_showcase tests passing.","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:00:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nkmi.6","title":"Layout optimization showcase + metrics","description":"What
- Expose layout optimization modes and present layout quality metrics.

Requirements
- Layout mode cycle (Auto/Dense/Spacious).
- Display layout solver metrics: iterations, objective score, constraint violations, fallback tier/reason.

Acceptance
- Metrics update on each re-layout; mode changes are obvious in UI.

 {"id":"bd-2nkmi.7","title":"Compose UI layout + theming for the new screen","description":"What
- Build the screen UI layout and theme integration.

Required regions
- Header (title, sample index, layout/fidelity, status)
- Sample list (full list or collapsed selector)
- Render viewport (dominant region)
- Control panel + Metrics panel
- Footer hints
- Optional status log panel for large size

Responsive behavior
- 80x24: collapsed sample selector + compact metrics line; control overlay toggle.
- 120x40: 3-column layout with controls+metrics on right.
- 200x60: expanded right column with metrics + status log.

Acceptance
- Layout is legible in all target sizes and consistent with demo theme.

-{"id":"bd-2nkmi.8","title":"Performance/telemetry overlay for mermaid screen","description":"What
- Implement the performance/telemetry overlay for Mermaid screen.

Metrics capture
- parse_ms, layout_ms, render_ms, cache hits, iteration counts.
- Frame budget warnings with color thresholds (good/ok/bad).

Logging
- Emit structured, test-friendly logs (JSONL or similar) including sample name, timings, layout mode, fidelity.
- Ensure logs are easy to validate in tests/e2e with clear failure output.

Acceptance
- Metrics are accurate, updated in-frame, and logged for test validation.

 {"id":"bd-2nkmi.9","title":"Unit + snapshot tests for Mermaid showcase screen","description":"What
- Add comprehensive unit + snapshot tests for the new screen.

Unit tests
- State transitions, command mapping, render-trigger conditions, error handling.

Snapshot tests
- 3+ sizes (80x24, 120x40, 200x60) x 3+ diagram types.
- Include panel toggle states and at least one error case sample.

Logging requirements
- Tests must emit readable diagnostics on failure (sample name, mode, metrics).

Acceptance
- New tests are deterministic and cover all interactive controls.

 {"id":"bd-2nu8","title":"Comprehensive test coverage + no-mock policy + e2e logging","description":"# Epic: Comprehensive test coverage + no-mock policy + E2E logging

## Goal
Guarantee correctness with **realistic, no-mock** unit tests and **end-to-end integration** coverage that produces rich, actionable logs on failure.

## Scope
- Unit coverage meets or exceeds targets in docs/testing/coverage-matrix.md (per crate/module).
- Tests use real components (PTY, Buffer/Frame, parser state machines). **No mocks/fakes**.
- E2E suite covers all critical terminal flows with structured artifacts for fast diagnosis.

## Deliverables
- Coverage report (per crate/module) + gap list mapped to tasks.
- Explicit no-mock policy + enforcement (inventory + CI guard).
- Expanded E2E scripts covering missing terminal flows.
- Hardened E2E logging (per-case logs, JSON summary, PTY dumps, durations).
- CI gating for coverage thresholds + E2E pass/fail.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Coverage targets achieved for each crate in the matrix.
- No mock/fake usage remains; enforcement prevents regressions.
- E2E suite covers: inline, alt-screen, input, ANSI, unicode, **mouse SGR, focus events, bracketed paste, kitty keyboard, resize/scroll-region, OSC 8 hyperlinks, mux behavior**.
- On failure: logs include env snapshot, per-case duration, PTY capture, hex + printable dump, and reproduction command.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T02:39:08.495111806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T18:16:03.731589013Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T18:16:03.731563104Z","close_reason":"All 18 child tasks closed. Comprehensive test coverage achieved: 1300+ unit tests across all crates (ftui-render 122, ftui-core 147, ftui-widgets 678, ftui-runtime 163, ftui-layout 75, ftui-text 183, ftui-style 22, ftui-extras 283+). 60+ E2E scenarios covering terminal flows. No-mock policy enforced via CI. Coverage tooling with llvm-cov + Codecov. E2E logging with PTY capture and JSON summaries.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2nu8.1","title":"Audit unit test coverage vs coverage matrix","description":"Run cargo llvm-cov for workspace/all-targets (and feature-gated modules as applicable) and map per-crate/module coverage to docs/testing/coverage-matrix.md.

Deliverables:
- docs/testing/coverage-report.md with a table: crate/module → current % → target % → pass/fail.
- A gap list that maps each unchecked matrix item to a test task (or confirms already covered).
- Notes for feature-gated code (which features enabled, any gaps and why).

Acceptance:
- Report is reproducible (exact command(s) listed).
- Every matrix checkbox has an explicit status (covered, missing, or deferred with rationale).
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T02:39:27.119492655Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:58:37.793969651Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:58:37.793950926Z","close_reason":"Completed: coverage-gap-report.md and coverage-matrix.md updated with cargo llvm-cov results","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nu8.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2nu8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3sa7.4","title":"Snapshot/Time Travel Player — E2E PTY Tests (Verbose Logs)","description":"PTY E2E tests with detailed logs for Snapshot/Time Travel Player, including timing/interaction traces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Snapshot/Time Travel Player — E2E PTY Tests (Verbose Logs)
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)

 {"id":"bd-3t87","title":"Progressive Rendering for Large Content with Virtualization","description":"# Progressive Rendering for Large Content with Virtualization

## Background & Motivation

Agent harnesses accumulate massive amounts of data over long sessions:
- Tool outputs can be thousands of lines
- LLM responses stream continuously
- Log files grow unbounded

Naively rendering all content causes O(n) render time, making the UI unusable.

**Why this matters:**
- Agent sessions can run for hours, accumulating 100K+ log lines
- Users expect smooth scrolling regardless of content size
- Memory usage must be bounded
- Render time must be O(visible) not O(total)

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Enhances bd-29v (Viewport/LogViewer) with virtualization
- Works with bd-1iqn (Render Budget) for graceful degradation

## Technical Design

### Core Types

```rust
/// Virtualized content container
pub struct Virtualized<T> {
    /// All items (or reference to external storage)
    items: VirtualizedStorage<T>,
    /// Current scroll offset (in items)
    scroll_offset: usize,
    /// Number of visible items (computed from area height)
    visible_count: usize,
    /// Overscan: extra items rendered above/below visible
    overscan: usize,
    /// Height per item (fixed or measured)
    item_height: ItemHeight,
    /// Scroll velocity for momentum
    scroll_velocity: f32,
}

pub enum VirtualizedStorage<T> {
    /// Owned vec of items
    Owned(Vec<T>),
    /// Reference to external storage with len
    External { len: usize, fetch: Box<dyn Fn(Range<usize>) -> Vec<T>> },
}

pub enum ItemHeight {
    /// All items have fixed height
    Fixed(u16),
    /// Items have variable height, measured lazily
    Variable(HeightCache),
}

/// LRU cache for measured item heights
pub struct HeightCache {
    cache: LruCache<usize, u16>,
    default_height: u16,
}
```

### Virtualized List Widget

```rust
impl<T: RenderItem> Widget for VirtualizedList<T> {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        // Calculate visible range
        let (start, end) = self.visible_range(area.height);
        
        // Apply overscan
        let render_start = start.saturating_sub(self.overscan);
        let render_end = (end + self.overscan).min(self.len());
        
        // Render only visible items
        let mut y = area.y;
        for idx in render_start..render_end {
            if let Some(item) = self.get(idx) {
                let item_height = self.item_height(idx);
                let item_area = Rect::new(area.x, y, area.width, item_height);
                
                if item_area.bottom() > area.y && item_area.top() < area.bottom() {
                    item.render(item_area, buf);
                }
                
                y += item_height;
            }
        }
        
        // Render scrollbar
        if self.len() > self.visible_count {
            self.render_scrollbar(area, buf);
        }
    }
}

impl<T> VirtualizedList<T> {
    fn visible_range(&self, height: u16) -> (usize, usize) {
        let start = self.scroll_offset;
        let visible = match &self.item_height {
            ItemHeight::Fixed(h) => (height / h) as usize,
            ItemHeight::Variable(cache) => {
                // Sum heights until we exceed viewport
                let mut count = 0;
                let mut total_height = 0u16;
                while total_height < height && start + count < self.len() {
                    total_height += cache.get_or_default(start + count);
                    count += 1;
                }
                count
            }
        };
        (start, start + visible)
    }
}
```

### Scroll Handling

```rust
impl<T> VirtualizedList<T> {
    /// Scroll by delta (positive = down)
    pub fn scroll(&mut self, delta: i32) {
        let new_offset = (self.scroll_offset as i32 + delta)
            .max(0)
            .min(self.len().saturating_sub(1) as i32);
        self.scroll_offset = new_offset as usize;
    }
    
    /// Scroll to specific item
    pub fn scroll_to(&mut self, idx: usize) {
        self.scroll_offset = idx.min(self.len().saturating_sub(1));
    }
    
    /// Scroll to bottom (follow mode for logs)
    pub fn scroll_to_bottom(&mut self) {
        if self.len() > self.visible_count {
            self.scroll_offset = self.len() - self.visible_count;
        }
    }
    
    /// Enable follow mode (auto-scroll on new items)
    pub fn set_follow(&mut self, follow: bool) {
        self.follow_mode = follow;
    }
    
    /// Called when items are added
    pub fn on_items_added(&mut self, count: usize) {
        if self.follow_mode {
            self.scroll_to_bottom();
        }
    }
}
```

### Momentum Scrolling

```rust
impl<T> VirtualizedList<T> {
    /// Apply scroll velocity (for smooth momentum)
    pub fn tick(&mut self, dt: Duration) {
        if self.scroll_velocity.abs() > 0.1 {
            let delta = (self.scroll_velocity * dt.as_secs_f32()) as i32;
            self.scroll(delta);
            
            // Apply friction
            self.scroll_velocity *= 0.95;
        } else {
            self.scroll_velocity = 0.0;
        }
    }
    
    /// Start momentum scroll
    pub fn fling(&mut self, velocity: f32) {
        self.scroll_velocity = velocity;
    }
}
```

### Memory-Efficient Log Storage

```rust
/// Log storage with bounded memory and optional persistence
pub struct LogRing {
    /// Circular buffer of log lines
    ring: VecDeque<LogLine>,
    /// Maximum lines in memory
    capacity: usize,
    /// Total lines ever received (for accurate scroll positions)
    total_count: usize,
    /// Optional overflow file for persistence
    overflow: Option<OverflowFile>,
}

impl LogRing {
    pub fn push(&mut self, line: LogLine) {
        self.total_count += 1;
        
        if self.ring.len() >= self.capacity {
            if let Some(overflow) = &mut self.overflow {
                // Write evicted line to overflow file
                overflow.write(self.ring.pop_front().unwrap());
            } else {
                self.ring.pop_front();
            }
        }
        
        self.ring.push_back(line);
    }
    
    pub fn get(&self, idx: usize) -> Option<&LogLine> {
        // Map absolute index to ring index
        let ring_start = self.total_count.saturating_sub(self.ring.len());
        if idx >= ring_start && idx < self.total_count {
            self.ring.get(idx - ring_start)
        } else if let Some(overflow) = &self.overflow {
            // Read from overflow (may involve I/O)
            overflow.read(idx)
        } else {
            None
        }
    }
}
```

### Integration with LogViewer (bd-29v)

```rust
pub struct LogViewer {
    /// Virtualized storage for log lines
    logs: Virtualized<LogLine>,
    /// Whether to follow new output
    follow: bool,
    /// Filter pattern
    filter: Option<Regex>,
    /// Syntax highlighter (optional)
    highlighter: Option<SyntaxHighlighter>,
}

impl LogViewer {
    pub fn append(&mut self, line: LogLine) {
        self.logs.push(line);
        if self.follow {
            self.logs.scroll_to_bottom();
        }
    }
    
    pub fn append_batch(&mut self, lines: impl Iterator<Item = LogLine>) {
        for line in lines {
            self.logs.push(line);
        }
        if self.follow {
            self.logs.scroll_to_bottom();
        }
    }
}
```

## Test Plan

### Unit Tests
- [ ] visible_range() computes correct bounds
- [ ] scroll() respects bounds (no negative, no past end)
- [ ] scroll_to_bottom() positions correctly
- [ ] follow mode auto-scrolls on push
- [ ] LogRing eviction works correctly
- [ ] Height cache returns defaults for unmeasured

### Integration Tests
- [ ] 100K items render in <10ms
- [ ] Scrollbar position is accurate
- [ ] Overscan prevents visible gaps during fast scroll
- [ ] Memory stays bounded with large content

### Property Tests
- [ ] scroll_offset always valid for current len()
- [ ] visible_range always within bounds
- [ ] LogRing capacity never exceeded

### E2E Tests
```bash
# Generate 100K log lines
./ftui-harness --test-mode log-stress --line-count 100000
# Assert: render time < 20ms
# Assert: memory < 50MB
# Assert: smooth scroll (no visible gaps)
```

## Performance Requirements
- Render time: O(visible), not O(total)
- Memory: Bounded by capacity config
- Scroll latency: < 16ms for 60fps feel

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Virtualized<T> container implemented
- [ ] VirtualizedList widget renders only visible
- [ ] LogRing provides bounded storage
- [ ] Scrollbar shows accurate position
- [ ] Follow mode works for streaming logs
- [ ] Momentum scrolling smooth
- [ ] 100K items work smoothly
- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"## Logging Requirements

```rust

    offset = scroll_offset,
    visible_range = ?(start, end),
    total_items = len,
    "Scroll position changed"
);

// Cache eviction (DEBUG level)

    evicted_count = count,
    ring_capacity = capacity,
    "LogRing evicted oldest items"
);

// Follow mode toggle (INFO level)
tracing::info!(follow = enabled, "Follow mode toggled");

// Render stats (TRACE level)
tracing::trace!(
    visible_items = visible_count,
    total_items = len,
    render_time_us = render_time.as_micros(),
    "VirtualizedList render complete"
);

// Memory stats (DEBUG level, periodic)
tracing::debug!(
    items_in_memory = ring.len(),
    memory_bytes = ring.memory_size(),
    "LogRing memory stats"
);
```

 {"id":"bd-3tfu","title":"Implement RenderBudget struct with time tracking","description":"# Implement RenderBudget struct with time tracking

## Background
Part of Render Budget Enforcement (bd-1iqn). The RenderBudget struct tracks elapsed time during frame rendering and manages degradation level.

## Technical Design

```rust
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};

/// Render time budget with graceful degradation
pub struct RenderBudget {
    /// Total time budget for this frame
    total: Duration,
    /// When this frame started rendering
    start: Instant,
    /// Current degradation level
    degradation: DegradationLevel,
    /// Per-phase budgets (optional breakdown)
    phases: PhaseBudgets,
}

/// Budget allocation per render phase
pub struct PhaseBudgets {
    /// Budget for widget tree traversal
    pub traverse: Duration,
    /// Budget for diff computation
    pub diff: Duration,
    /// Budget for ANSI emission
    pub present: Duration,
}

impl RenderBudget {
    /// Create a budget with the given total time
    pub fn new(total: Duration) -> Self {
        Self {
            total,
            start: Instant::now(),
            degradation: DegradationLevel::Full,
            phases: PhaseBudgets::default(),
        }
    }
    
    /// Time remaining in this frame budget
    #[inline]
    pub fn remaining(&self) -> Duration {
        self.total.saturating_sub(self.start.elapsed())
    }
    
    /// Check if we should degrade before an expensive operation
    /// estimated_cost: rough estimate of how long the operation takes
    #[inline]
    pub fn should_degrade(&self, estimated_cost: Duration) -> bool {
        self.remaining() < estimated_cost
    }
    
    /// Advance to next degradation level
    pub fn degrade(&mut self) {
        let old = self.degradation;
        self.degradation = self.degradation.degrade();
        tracing::warn!(
            from = ?old,
            to = ?self.degradation,
            elapsed_ms = self.start.elapsed().as_millis(),
            "Degrading render fidelity"
        );
    }
    
    /// Get current degradation level for widgets to check
    #[inline]
    pub fn degradation(&self) -> DegradationLevel {
        self.degradation
    }
    
    /// Check if budget is exhausted (should skip frame)
    #[inline]
    pub fn exhausted(&self) -> bool {
        self.remaining().is_zero() || self.degradation.skip_frame()
    }
    
    /// Record phase entry for detailed timing
    pub fn enter_phase(&mut self, phase: RenderPhase) {
        // For tracing spans
    }
    
    /// Get elapsed time since frame start
    #[inline]
    pub fn elapsed(&self) -> Duration {
        self.start.elapsed()
    }
}

impl Default for PhaseBudgets {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            traverse: Duration::from_millis(4),
            diff: Duration::from_millis(2),
            present: Duration::from_millis(4),
        }
    }
}
```

## Why These Defaults
- Total 16ms = ~60fps feel
- 4ms traverse: widget tree can be complex
- 2ms diff: typically fast with good cell comparison
- 4ms present: includes IO overhead
- Remaining ~6ms: buffer for variance

## Test Requirements
- [ ] remaining() decreases as time passes
- [ ] should_degrade() returns true when cost > remaining
- [ ] exhausted() when remaining is zero OR degradation is SkipFrame
- [ ] degrade() logs warning via tracing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] RenderBudget struct implemented
- [ ] PhaseBudgets with sensible defaults
- [ ] Time tracking is accurate
- [ ] All methods are #[inline] for hot path","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:20.004168393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:17:46.980081123Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:17:46.980059762Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of bd-1iqn in budget.rs - RenderBudget with remaining(), should_degrade(), degrade(), upgrade(), reset(), next_frame()","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3tfu","depends_on_id":"bd-cyww","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
